response.justification: While Maria demonstrates exceptional software engineering breadth and strong architectural thinking across 15+ years, she is fundamentally misaligned with this role's requirements. Her C++ experience is limited to 6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and thesis work, with virtually no exposure in the past decade. She lacks critical domain expertise in industrial automation, IoT, embedded systems, and security standards (IEC 62443, SDL), and her recent 5+ year career trajectory has been entirely within the JavaScript/TypeScript/web ecosystem. Despite her obvious capability to learn, the gaps are too significant for a mid-senior C++ role requiring deep domain fluency from day one.
strengths: Proven ability to architect and design complex systems across diverse domains (microservices at Dunnhumby, GIS platform, event-driven infrastructure)
Strong mentoring, teaching, and knowledge-sharing track record (organized code dojos, created training courses, open-source contributions)
Extensive software craftsmanship with demonstrated expertise in modern development practices (TDD, CI/CD, agile methodologies, infrastructure automation)
concerns: Critically weak C++ expertise—only 6 months of recent hands-on experience (2014–2015); role requires 8–12 years of deep C++ and STL mastery
Zero demonstrated experience in industrial automation, IoT, embedded systems, or industrial protocols (Modbus, IEC standards, OPC UA); no background in secure development or IEC 62443 compliance
Recent 5+ year career entirely web-focused (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB); significant ramp-up required to rebuild embedded systems and low-level systems thinking

key_factors.factor: C++ expertise gap (6 months vs. 8-12 years required)
Industrial automation and IoT domain experience
Overall software engineering capability and learning track record
